name: Checkbox
description: Checkbox captures an independent multi-select choice — table row selection, opt-in lists, select-none/some/all groups. It's a bare control (no built-in label): pair it with @mui/material/FormControlLabel for a clickable label.
keyRule: Use Checkbox for select none/some/all, applied whenever the surrounding form/table applies it (usually on submit, but a header 'select all' checkbox can apply immediately to a live selection). Don't use it for exactly-one-of-many (use Radio) or an instant on/off setting (use Switch).
correction: One of three real components split out of the old MDC-modeled 'SelectionControl' umbrella, which documented Checkbox, Radio, and Switch behind a single fake `type` discriminator prop. The real library has three separate components with three separate, non-overlapping prop APIs — see Radio.doc.json and Switch.doc.json for the other two.

variants.name: Standalone checkbox
variants.whenToUse: A single independent yes/no choice in a form (e.g. "I agree to terms").

variants.name: List/table row checkbox
variants.whenToUse: Multi-select rows in a list or DataGrid; each row's Checkbox is independent of the others.

variants.name: Header select-all checkbox (indeterminate)
variants.whenToUse: A parent checkbox that selects/clears an entire group and shows `indeterminate` when only some rows are checked.

anatomy.name: Root
anatomy.description: a ButtonBase-backed clickable area (renders as a button element) containing the visible icon and a visually-hidden native `<input type="checkbox">`.

anatomy.name: Icon (unchecked)
anatomy.description: default CheckBoxOutlineBlankIcon; override via the `icon` prop.

anatomy.name: Checked icon
anatomy.description: default CheckBoxIcon; override via the `checkedIcon` prop.

anatomy.name: Indeterminate icon
anatomy.description: default IndeterminateCheckBoxIcon (a dash), shown when `indeterminate` is true regardless of `checked`; override via `indeterminateIcon`.

anatomy.name: Label (external)
anatomy.description: not part of Checkbox itself. Wrap in @mui/material/FormControlLabel (`<FormControlLabel control={<Checkbox />} label="..." />`) to get a clickable label — the old SelectionControl's built-in `label` prop has no real equivalent on Checkbox.

behaviors.name: Controlled vs uncontrolled
behaviors.description: Use `checked`+`onChange` for a controlled checkbox (typical for table/list selection state you own), or `defaultChecked` for an uncontrolled one.

behaviors.name: Indeterminate is display-only
behaviors.description: Setting `indeterminate` only changes the rendered icon and sets a `data-indeterminate` attribute — it does NOT set the native DOM `indeterminate` property (cross-browser inconsistency), and clicking an indeterminate checkbox does not automatically select/clear a group. The consumer (e.g. a table header) must compute `indeterminate`/`checked` from its own selection state and handle onChange to select/clear all rows itself.

behaviors.name: Apply timing
behaviors.description: Not enforced by the component. A row-selection Checkbox usually applies its selection immediately to in-memory state; whether that selection is then submitted, exported, or bulk-actioned is a decision made by the surrounding UI, not Checkbox.

states.treatment: Dash icon, independent of the underlying `checked` value.
ButtonBase ripple/state-layer around the icon; visible focus ring.

specs.value: size="small" — set at the theme level (theme.ts: MuiCheckbox.defaultProps.size), not by this wrapper. MUI's own default is "medium"; Cartrack's theme overrides it globally.
medium ≈24px icon with a larger click/touch target via internal padding; small (Cartrack default) ≈20px icon with a smaller target — MUI internals, not tokenized.
not Checkbox's own concern — FormControlLabel controls the gap between the control and its label.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: Shows the indeterminate (dash) icon regardless of `checked`. Display-only — does not set the native DOM indeterminate property or auto-manage a group; see 'Indeterminate is display-only' above.

props.name: indeterminateIcon
props.description: Icon shown when indeterminate. Default: IndeterminateCheckBoxIcon.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Fired when the checked state changes; `event.target.checked` also carries the new value.

props.name: size
props.description: Default 'medium' in stock MUI, overridden to 'small' fleet-wide by theme.ts.

props.name: disableRipple
props.description: Disables the hover/press ripple. Default false.

props.name: required
props.description: Marks the underlying native input as required.

props.name: name
props.description: Name attribute of the native input.

props.name: value
props.description: Value attribute of the native input (cast to string by the DOM).

props.name: id
props.description: id of the native input — pair with FormControlLabel's htmlFor, or set it directly for a manual <label>.

doThis: Use FormControlLabel to give every Checkbox a clickable label — there is no built-in label prop.
Compute `indeterminate` and the select-all `checked`/`onChange` yourself from the group's real selection state.
Make disabled state clear (theme's disabled opacity handles this automatically).
dontDoThis: Don't expect `indeterminate` to set the native DOM indeterminate property or auto-select a group — it's icon-only.
Don't use Checkbox for exactly-one-of-many (use Radio inside a RadioGroup).
Don't rely on colour alone to show selection — the icon shape itself (outline vs filled vs dash) already conveys state.
accessibility: Renders a real native `<input type="checkbox">` (visually hidden) so screen readers get correct semantics for free.
Always pair with FormControlLabel (or your own <label htmlFor>) so the label is announced and clickable.

Indeterminate state is exposed via `data-indeterminate` and the visual dash icon — assistive tech support for aria-checked="mixed" varies, so also convey partial-selection count in visible text where it matters (e.g. "3 of 10 selected").
Focus is visible via the theme's focus ring.

related.name: Radio
related.why: Exactly one choice from a small set — split from the same old SelectionControl umbrella

related.name: Switch
related.why: Instant on/off setting — split from the same old SelectionControl umbrella
